purpose: Scale-control for SAE seed-stability finding: re-run layer6-512 condition at 30x token scale to test whether cross-seed instability is a low-data artifact.
conclusion: Cross-seed feature instability is NOT purely a low-data artifact. Removing the dead-feature confound (65%->8.5%) and training on 30x more data raises the live-only median best-match cosine from 0.257 to 0.472, but it remains far below the 0.9 'same feature' threshold (only 4.33% of features cross it). Reproducibility improves monotonically with scale; whether it reaches 0.9 at production scale (1M-1B tokens) is untested and is the key open question.
